explicit_text: I grew up in Madison, Wisconsin, after my family moved there in the late 1960s. We lived at Truax Field, which is now where the MATC parking lot is. Madison was very different back then; East Towne Mall and West Towne Mall weren't built yet, and it was mostly farmland around. Shopping was mainly downtown at places like Gimbels, Manchester's, and Rennebaum's. I went to elementary and high school there before joining the military.

Shopping was mainly downtown at places like Gimbels, Manchester's, and Rennebaum's. I went to elementary and high school there before joining the military.

explicit_text: I was actually born in Japan, at Johnson Air Base in Tokyo, because my dad was stationed there in the Air Force. Although I was only one year old when we left, I always wanted to go back to Japan, and when I joined the military, I was stationed at Misawa Air Base. It was a dream come true to have my first dorm there, and my daughter was even born in Japan in 1998. Japan holds a special place in my life because of my family's military ties.

explicit_text: My dad was in the United States Air Force, which influenced my life greatly. I followed in his footsteps and enlisted myself, first in the Air National Guard in 1980 and then active duty in 1985. I was proud to be one of the first black females in the 115th Fighter Wing, and my military career took me to places like Misawa Air Base in Japan. The Air Force shaped much of my identity and life experiences.

explicit_text: I went through basic training twice at Lackland Air Force Base in Texas. The first time was when I enlisted active duty in 1985, even though I had already been in the Guard. I earned an oak leaf cluster on my graduation ribbon because of that second basic training. Lackland was where I experienced both the fun of drum and bugle corps and the tougher side of basic training the second time around.

explicit_text: During my first basic training at Lackland, I was recruited into the drum and bugle corps, which was like the Air Force band but with drum corps instruments. It made basic training more fun and laid back for me, and I got to perform at graduations and parades. It was a unique and proud part of my military experience.
